content.problemStatement:
You are given a sequence A1,A2,‚Ä¶,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1‚Äč,A2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äč. You should select a (not necessarily contiguous) subsequence of AAA and reverse it. In other words, if you select a subsequence Ai1,Ai2,‚Ä¶,AiKA_{i_1}, A_{i_2}, \ldots, A_{i_K}Ai1‚Äč‚Äč,Ai2‚Äč‚Äč,‚Ä¶,AiK‚Äč‚Äč (1‚Č§i1<‚Ä¶<iK‚Č§N1 \le i_1 \lt \ldots \lt i_K \le N1‚Č§i1‚Äč<‚Ä¶<iK‚Äč‚Č§N), then for each valid jjj, the iji_jij‚Äč-th element of the resulting sequence is equal to the iK+1‚ąíji_{K+1-j}iK+1‚ąíj‚Äč-th element of the original sequence; all other elements are the same as in the original sequence.

In the resulting sequence, you want to calculate the maximum sum of a contiguous subsequence (possibly an empty sequence, with sum 000). Find its maximum possible value and a subsequence which you should select in order to obtain this maximum value. If there are multiple solutions, you may find any one of them.
constraints: the sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 2‚čÖ1062 \cdot 10^62‚čÖ106
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,‚Ä¶,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1‚Äč,A2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äč.
content.outputFormat:
For each test case, print two lines.


The first of these lines should contain a single integer ‚Äē the maximum possible sum of a contiguous subsequence.
The second line should contain an integer KKK followed by a space and KKK space-separated integers i1,i2,‚Ä¶,iKi_1, i_2, \ldots, i_Ki1‚Äč,i2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,iK‚Äč.
